elements.name: Model Discovery and Identification
elements.description: Can a user or system discover and uniquely identify this model?

sub_elements.name: Persistent Identifier
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.references: ['https://github.com/lbnl-climate/climatenet-v2', 'https://climatenet.lbl.gov']
sub_elements.quality_note: GitHub repository and project landing page URLs provided as persistent identifiers

semantic_analysis.format_details: Both URLs are well-formed; no DOI present but GitHub + institutional landing page constitute persistent identification
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: References align with library_name 'climatenet' and LBNL affiliation
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: GitHub repo and lbl.gov subdomain are plausible LBNL hosting; no DOI reduces long-term persistence guarantees

sub_elements.name: Model Name and Description Completeness
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.name: 'ClimateNet-v2', short_description: 65 chars, overview: 327 chars with mission context
sub_elements.quality_note: Clear name, concise short description, and comprehensive overview explaining purpose and DOE mission alignment

semantic_analysis.format_details: Name follows convention; overview exceeds 200 chars; documentation field populated
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Overview describes climate weather event detection consistent with pipeline_tag='image-segmentation' and tags ['climate','extreme-weather','segmentation']
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: Description plausibly matches a ResNet-50 FPN segmentation model on atmospheric data

sub_elements.name: Tags / Pipeline Tag for Searchability
sub_elements.evidence: tags: 7 tags ['climate', 'extreme-weather', 'segmentation', 'object-detection', 'scientific-ml', 'doe', 'nersc'], pipeline_tag: 'image-segmentation'
sub_elements.quality_note: Excellent searchability with 7 relevant tags and appropriate pipeline_tag

semantic_analysis.format_details: pipeline_tag 'image-segmentation' is a recognized HuggingFace pipeline tag
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: All tags align with stated task (segmentation + detection of weather events) and infrastructure (NERSC, DOE)
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: Tags are concrete and discoverable on HF Hub / GitHub topic search

sub_elements.name: Model Landing Page or Repository URL
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.references[0]: 'https://github.com/lbnl-climate/climatenet-v2', references[1]: 'https://climatenet.lbl.gov'
sub_elements.quality_note: Both GitHub repository and dedicated project landing page provided

semantic_analysis.format_details: URLs well-formed; GitHub org 'lbnl-climate' and lbl.gov subdomain consistent with LBNL affiliation
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Repository URL also referenced from training_procedure.reproducibility_info.pipeline_url
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: LBNL is a real DOE national lab; URL structure is plausible

sub_elements.name: Library / Framework Identification
sub_elements.evidence: framework: 'PyTorch', framework_version: '2.1.0', library_name: 'climatenet'
sub_elements.quality_note: Complete framework identification with specific version

semantic_analysis.format_details: framework='PyTorch' paired with pinned framework_version='2.1.0' (no floating constraint)
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: compute_infrastructure.software confirms 'PyTorch 2.1.0 with CUDA 12.1'; software_dependencies pins 'pytorch=2.1.0=py310_cuda12.1_cudnn8.9.0_0'
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: PyTorch 2.1.0 was released October 2023, consistent with 2024-11-15 model release

elements.name: Model Access and Distribution
elements.description: Can the model weights, code, and inference pipeline be located and used?

sub_elements.name: Weight Distribution Mechanism Defined
sub_elements.evidence: usage_documentation.installation_instructions: 'wget https://climatenet.lbl.gov/models/climatenet-v2.0.0.pth' (650 MB)
sub_elements.quality_note: Clear download mechanism with explicit URL and file size

semantic_analysis.format_details: wget URL well-formed; .pth extension correctly identifies PyTorch state_dict
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Hosting domain (climatenet.lbl.gov) matches landing page in model_details.references
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: 650 MB checkpoint plausible for ResNet-50 FPN (~38.5M params × 4 bytes ≈ 154MB; with optimizer state/extras ~650MB is reasonable for an exported checkpoint)

sub_elements.name: Code Repository Available
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.references[0]: 'https://github.com/lbnl-climate/climatenet-v2', training_procedure.reproducibility_info.pipeline_url: 'https://github.com/lbnl-climate/climatenet-v2/blob/main/training/train.py'
sub_elements.quality_note: GitHub repository with explicit training script path provided

semantic_analysis.format_details: GitHub URLs well-formed; pipeline_url points to a specific file in main branch
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Pipeline URL matches references[0] base path
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: GitHub org/repo and file path layout is conventional

sub_elements.name: Inference API / Usage Example Provided
sub_elements.evidence: usage_documentation.code_examples: 3 complete examples (basic inference, evaluation, batch processing) with runnable Python code
sub_elements.quality_note: Comprehensive usage examples with complete, runnable code snippets

semantic_analysis.format_details: Examples include language tags (python, bash) and runnable code
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Code example uses (16, 768, 768) patch shape that matches input_format spec; outputs dict keys ('segmentation', 'detections') match output_format
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: ClimateNetV2.from_pretrained API and SLURM batch script use plausible NERSC conventions (m1234 account, /pscratch paths)

sub_elements.name: Input / Output Specification
sub_elements.evidence: model_parameters.input_format: 'Shape: (768, 768, 16), Channels: T, U, V, Q, Z, PSL, TMQ, PRECT, Z-score normalized, HDF5 format'; output_format: 'Segmentation mask: (768, 768) uint8, Bounding boxes: (x1, y1, x2, y2, class_id, confidence), HDF5 or GeoTIFF'
sub_elements.quality_note: Complete and detailed input/output specifications with shapes, data types, normalization, and file formats

semantic_analysis.format_details: Tensor shapes, dtypes, normalization scheme, and file containers all specified
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Input is described as (768,768,16) channels-last but code example uses (16,768,768) channels-first PyTorch convention — minor inconsistency between spec and code
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: 16 atmospheric variables and 4-class output (TC/AR/front/background) match the use case

sub_elements.name: Model File Format Specified
sub_elements.evidence: usage_documentation references '.pth' format (PyTorch state_dict), input/output use 'HDF5' and 'GeoTIFF'
sub_elements.quality_note: Model checkpoint format (.pth) and data formats (HDF5, GeoTIFF) explicitly stated

semantic_analysis.format_details: Standard PyTorch .pth extension; HDF5 and GeoTIFF are recognized scientific data formats
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Checkpoint format aligns with declared framework (PyTorch)
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: GeoTIFF support fits geospatial output use case

elements.name: Model Reuse and Interoperability
elements.description: Is sufficient information provided to reuse and integrate this model with others?

sub_elements.name: License Terms Allow Reuse
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.licenses[0].identifier: 'BSD-3-Clause', license_name: 'BSD 3-Clause License', license_link: 'https://opensource.org/licenses/BSD-3-Clause'
sub_elements.quality_note: Clear permissive license (BSD-3-Clause) with SPDX identifier and link

semantic_analysis.format_details: 'BSD-3-Clause' is a valid SPDX identifier
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: BSD-3-Clause is compatible with ResNet-50 ImageNet-pretrained weights (BSD/MIT-style) and with permissive PyTorch licensing
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: License link resolves to OSI canonical URL; appropriate for DOE national-lab open-source release

sub_elements.name: Standard Framework / Format Used
sub_elements.evidence: framework: 'PyTorch', framework_version: '2.1.0', compute_infrastructure.software: 'PyTorch 2.1.0 with CUDA 12.1, Horovod 0.28.1'
sub_elements.quality_note: Standard PyTorch framework with exact version and CUDA compatibility

semantic_analysis.format_details: Versions are pinned exactly (no '>=' floating constraints)
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: PyTorch 2.1.0 + CUDA 12.1 + Horovod 0.28.1 form a coherent stack; matches software_dependencies conda spec
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: Horovod 0.28.1 was current in 2023; matches Apex 23.08 release window

sub_elements.name: Base Model or Foundation Lineage Stated
sub_elements.evidence: model_parameters.model_architecture: 'ResNet-50 pretrained on ImageNet', training_procedure.reproducibility_info.hyperparameters.model_initialization: 'ResNet-50 ImageNet pretrained (modified first layer)'
sub_elements.quality_note: Base model (ResNet-50 ImageNet pretrained) clearly stated with modification details

semantic_analysis.format_details: Base model named in prose but top-level 'base_model' field is not populated
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Modification (16-channel input layer) is consistent with input_format (16 atmospheric variables)
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: ImageNet-pretrained ResNet-50 + first-conv replacement is a standard transfer-learning pattern for multi-channel scientific data

sub_elements.name: Supported Tasks Declared
sub_elements.evidence: pipeline_tag: 'image-segmentation', considerations.use_cases: 4 detailed use cases (TC tracking, AR detection, weather front identification, extreme event analysis)
sub_elements.quality_note: Primary task (image-segmentation) and specific scientific use cases clearly documented

semantic_analysis.format_details: pipeline_tag valid; use cases enumerated with descriptions
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Use cases align with pipeline_tag and tags (segmentation + detection of TC/AR/fronts)
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: Each use case maps directly to one of the 3 segmentation classes plus aggregate analysis

sub_elements.name: Reproducibility Artifacts Provided
sub_elements.evidence: training_procedure.reproducibility_info: seed=42, pipeline_url, hyperparameters (optimizer, lr, batch_size, epochs), environment_config; compute_infrastructure.software_dependencies: full conda environment spec; usage_documentation.training_configuration: complete SLURM command with all flags
sub_elements.quality_note: Comprehensive reproducibility artifacts: seed, hyperparameters, environment spec, training script, and SLURM configuration

semantic_analysis.format_details: Seed is integer; conda spec uses exact pins (pytorch=2.1.0=...); SLURM command includes all required flags
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Batch size 2/GPU × 256 GPUs = 512 effective batch matches stated 'global batch size 512' in training_speed
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: 40 epochs × 600 steps = 24,000 steps as stated; 3.2h/epoch × 40 = 128h total matches

elements.name: Ethical Use and Responsible AI
elements.description: Does the model card provide clear information about risks, bias, and ethical oversight?

sub_elements.name: Ethical Considerations Documented
sub_elements.evidence: considerations.ethical_considerations: 2 considerations with mitigation strategies ('Climate justice implications' with equitable performance validation, 'Model uncertainty communication' with confidence interval reporting)
sub_elements.quality_note: Named ethical risks with concrete mitigation strategies

semantic_analysis.format_details: Each entry has 'name' + 'mitigation_strategy' fields populated
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Uncertainty-communication mitigation aligns with reported confidence intervals in quantitative_analysis; climate-justice mitigation aligns with documented polar-region limitation
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: Both ethical risks are appropriate for a climate science model (geographic equity + uncertainty)

sub_elements.name: Known Model / Output Bias Disclosed
sub_elements.evidence: No bias_model or bias_output fields present
sub_elements.quality_note: Missing explicit bias disclosure. While limitations mention degraded performance in polar regions, no formal bias fields populated

semantic_analysis.format_details: bias_model and bias_output fields are absent
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: limitations[2] documents geographic bias ('degrades in polar regions due to limited training data at high latitudes >70°') but this is NOT mirrored in dedicated bias_* fields; structured bias metadata is missing
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: A segmentation model trained on northern-hemisphere-biased climate model output has clear sampling-bias signature that should be in bias_input/bias_model

sub_elements.name: Out-of-Scope and Discouraged Uses
sub_elements.evidence: considerations.out_of_scope_uses: 3 explicit out-of-scope uses (real-time weather forecasting, direct satellite application, climate change attribution)
sub_elements.quality_note: Clear out-of-scope uses with explanations of why each use case is inappropriate

semantic_analysis.format_details: 3 entries, each with descriptive rationale
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Out-of-scope items align with training data (climate model output, not observations) and with declared use cases
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: Each excluded use is a well-known misapplication risk for ML weather models

sub_elements.name: Sensitive Data Use Disclosed
sub_elements.evidence: model_parameters.data does not include sensitive.sensitive_data_used field
sub_elements.quality_note: No explicit sensitive data disclosure. Climate model output is typically non-sensitive, but formal field not populated

semantic_analysis.format_details: sensitive.sensitive_data_used field is absent from all 3 dataset entries
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Climate simulation output is plausibly non-PII, so absence of sensitive_data follow-on fields is internally consistent — but the boolean flag itself should be explicitly set to false rather than omitted
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: CAM5.1 / E3SM / MPAS-A are public climate simulation outputs with no PII; sensitive_data_used=false would be the plausible value

sub_elements.name: Intended Users and Stakeholder Impact Statement
sub_elements.evidence: considerations.users: 3 user groups (climate scientists, DOE researchers, ML researchers); considerations.tradeoffs: 2 tradeoffs (accuracy vs speed, model size vs accuracy)
sub_elements.quality_note: Named user groups with documented tradeoffs

semantic_analysis.format_details: users[] and tradeoffs[] both populated with descriptions
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Tradeoffs are technical (accuracy vs speed, size vs accuracy) rather than ethical (fairness vs accuracy); given the polar-region bias, an accuracy-vs-geographic-fairness tradeoff would also be expected
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: User groups are appropriate for a DOE-developed scientific ML model

elements.name: Model Architecture and Training Composition
elements.description: Can the model's architecture and training composition be understood from metadata?

sub_elements.name: Architecture Described in Detail
sub_elements.evidence: model_parameters.model_architecture: 350+ char detailed description including ResNet-50 backbone, FPN structure, segmentation head (4x upsampling, conv layers), detection head (RPN, ROI Align), total parameters: 38.5M
sub_elements.quality_note: Comprehensive layer-by-layer architecture description with parameter count

semantic_analysis.format_details: Multi-line block scalar with structured sub-sections (Backbone / FPN / Heads / Total params)
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: 16-channel modified first conv consistent with input_format (16 variables); 4-way softmax consistent with 4-class output (TC/AR/front/background)
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: 38.5M params is in the right range for ResNet-50 FPN with detection head (vanilla ResNet-50 ~25M, FPN+heads add ~10-15M)

sub_elements.name: Training Data Documented
sub_elements.evidence: model_parameters.data: 3 datasets (CAM5.1 High-Resolution, E3SM v2 High-Resolution, MPAS-A 15km) with names, descriptions, and links
sub_elements.quality_note: Concrete climate model datasets with links and resolution specifications

semantic_analysis.format_details: Each entry has name, description, link
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Training datasets (CAM5.1, E3SM v2, MPAS-A) overlap with declared evaluation benchmarks (E3SM v2, MPAS-A, GFDL CM4). Methodology states the split is by simulation year on the same model output, which is a legitimate temporal holdout but should be flagged as not a held-out-model evaluation
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: All 3 are real climate model families used in DOE/NCAR climate science

sub_elements.name: Hyperparameters Reported
sub_elements.evidence: training_procedure.reproducibility_info.hyperparameters: optimizer='AdamW', learning_rate=0.0001, batch_size=2, training_epochs=40, optimization_techniques (mixed precision, gradient clipping, cosine annealing, warmup)
sub_elements.quality_note: Complete hyperparameter specification including optimizer, learning rate, batch size, epochs, and optimization techniques

semantic_analysis.format_details: Numeric hyperparameters typed correctly; optimization_techniques is a list of strings
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: training_procedure.description says 'standard SGD optimization' but hyperparameters.optimizer is 'AdamW' — narrative description is inconsistent with the structured hyperparameter value
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: AdamW with lr=1e-4 and cosine annealing + warmup is a standard transfer-learning recipe

sub_elements.name: Compute Infrastructure Reported (Extended)
sub_elements.evidence: compute_infrastructure.hardware: 'NERSC Perlmutter, 64 nodes × 4 A100 40GB = 256 GPUs, AMD EPYC 7763, 512GB RAM, Slingshot-11'; training_speed: '32,768 GPU-hours (128 hours on 256 A100s)'
sub_elements.quality_note: Detailed compute infrastructure with GPU type/count, CPU, memory, interconnect, and total compute hours

semantic_analysis.format_details: hardware_list itemized; software lists framework versions; training_speed quantifies throughput and totals
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: 256 GPUs × 128h = 32,768 GPU-hours (matches stated value); AMD EPYC 7763 + Slingshot-11 are Perlmutter's actual specs
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: NERSC Perlmutter has 1,792 A100 GPUs; 256-GPU allocations are routine. 38.5M-param model with 40 epochs in 128h at 2,100 patches/s is reasonable

sub_elements.name: Training / Evaluation Split Defined
sub_elements.evidence: training_procedure.methodology: 'Train/val/test split: 70/15/15 by simulation year'; quantitative_analysis.evaluation_procedure: 'Test set: 3 climate models × 10 years, Event counts: 1,247 TCs, 3,582 ARs, 8,921 fronts'
sub_elements.quality_note: Clear train/val/test split with detailed evaluation dataset composition

semantic_analysis.format_details: Percentages and event counts explicit
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Train and test draw from overlapping model families (E3SM v2, MPAS-A) but separated by simulation year — temporal holdout is legitimate but cross-model generalization is partially conflated with within-model holdout (only GFDL CM4 is fully out-of-distribution at the model level)
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: Event counts (1247 TCs over 30 simulation-years) are consistent with observed global TC frequency (~80/yr) when accounting for multiple models

elements.name: Model Provenance and Versioning
elements.description: Can a user determine model versions, update history, and provenance?

sub_elements.name: Version Number Provided
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.version.name: 'v2.0.0'
sub_elements.quality_note: Semantic version number provided

semantic_analysis.format_details: 'v2.0.0' matches semver MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH with 'v' prefix
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Matches model name suffix 'ClimateNet-v2' and checkpoint filename 'climatenet-v2.0.0.pth'
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: v2.0.0 implies a prior v1; version.diff references upgrades from ResNet-34 (v1) to ResNet-50

sub_elements.name: Version Date Documented
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.version.date: '2024-11-15', version.last_updated: '2024-11-20T14:30:00Z'
sub_elements.quality_note: Both release date and last updated timestamp provided in ISO 8601 format

semantic_analysis.format_details: ISO 8601 date and datetime formats both valid
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: last_updated (2024-11-20) is after release date (2024-11-15), as expected
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: Dates align with SC24 conference timeframe (Nov 17-22, 2024 in Atlanta) referenced in the citation

sub_elements.name: Change Description for This Version
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.version.diff: 'Upgraded to ResNet-50 from ResNet-34, Added atmospheric river detection capability, Improved tropical cyclone boundary accuracy by 15%, Trained on 3x more data (12 vs 4 climate models)'
sub_elements.quality_note: Detailed change description with specific improvements and quantitative metrics

semantic_analysis.format_details: Multi-line block scalar with 4 itemized changes
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Diff says 'trained on 12 climate models' but model_parameters.data only enumerates 3 (CAM5.1, E3SM v2, MPAS-A); discrepancy unresolved
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: +15% TC boundary accuracy and architecture upgrade are plausible v1→v2 improvements

sub_elements.name: Owners / Contributors Identified
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.contributors: 4 contributors with roles (developed_by, contributed_by, maintained_by, funded_by), names, emails, ORCIDs, affiliations (LBNL, ANL, DOE Office of Science)
sub_elements.quality_note: Comprehensive contributor attribution with CRediT-style roles, contact information, and ORCIDs

semantic_analysis.format_details: Emails are RFC-5322 valid (jane.doe@lbl.gov, jsmith@anl.gov, climate-ai@lbl.gov); ORCIDs match orcid.org/XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX pattern
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Email domains (lbl.gov, anl.gov) match listed affiliations (LBNL, ANL); CRediT roles cover authorship + maintenance + funding
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: ORCIDs 0000-0002-1234-5678 and 0000-0003-9876-5432 appear to be sequential placeholder IDs (common test patterns) rather than real registered ORCIDs — likely template placeholders for this example card

sub_elements.name: Citation or BibTeX Provided
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.citations[0]: style='IEEE', full citation for SC24 conference paper
sub_elements.quality_note: IEEE-style citation provided with conference details

semantic_analysis.format_details: IEEE-style narrative citation provided but no BibTeX entry and no DOI/URL to paper
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Authors 'J. Doe, J. Smith' map to contributors[0] and [1]; year 2024 matches version.date
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: SC24 (Supercomputing 2024) is a real venue published by IEEE/ACM; conference paper format is plausible

elements.name: Training and Evaluation Transparency
elements.description: Can training and evaluation procedures be replicated or understood?

sub_elements.name: Training Procedure Documented
sub_elements.evidence: training_procedure.methodology: 5-step detailed procedure (data preparation, distributed training, loss function, regularization, validation) with specific techniques (weighted cross-entropy, gradient accumulation, learning rate scaling)
sub_elements.quality_note: Comprehensive training procedure with data preparation, distributed training strategy, loss functions, and regularization

semantic_analysis.format_details: Multi-step methodology with structured sub-sections
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: training_procedure.description states 'standard SGD optimization' while hyperparameters.optimizer is 'AdamW' (same inconsistency noted in Element 5)
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: Loss weighting (λ_seg=1.0, λ_det=0.5) and weighted cross-entropy for class imbalance are standard for multi-task segmentation+detection

sub_elements.name: Evaluation Procedure Documented
sub_elements.evidence: quantitative_analysis.evaluation_procedure: metrics (IoU for segmentation, mAP for detection), benchmarks (3 climate models × 10 years, event counts), baselines (ClimateNet-v1, TECA), uncertainty quantification (bootstrap with 10,000 iterations)
sub_elements.quality_note: Detailed evaluation procedure with metrics, benchmarks, baselines, and uncertainty quantification

semantic_analysis.format_details: All sub-fields of evaluation_procedure populated (description, benchmarks, baselines, sota_comparison, uncertainty_quantification)
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Bootstrap with 10,000 iterations matches reported CI widths (±0.006-0.010); evaluation_data_separate=true is consistent with held-out simulation years
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: TECA (Toolkit for Extreme Climate Analysis) is a real reference baseline; ensemble σ=0.012 across 5 seeds is plausible

sub_elements.name: Reproducibility Information (Extended)
sub_elements.evidence: training_procedure.reproducibility_info: random_seed=42, environment_config with conda spec, pipeline_url to training script, complete hyperparameters
sub_elements.quality_note: Complete reproducibility information with seed, environment specification, and training script link

semantic_analysis.format_details: All recommended fields populated; conda spec uses fully pinned versions including build strings
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Seed=42 referenced both in reproducibility_info and in the SLURM srun command in usage_documentation.training_configuration
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: No container hash / no deterministic-cudnn flag mentioned; non-deterministic GPU ops may still cause minor variation despite seed

sub_elements.name: Open-Source Code Linked
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.references[0]: 'https://github.com/lbnl-climate/climatenet-v2', reproducibility_info.pipeline_url: 'https://github.com/lbnl-climate/climatenet-v2/blob/main/training/train.py'
sub_elements.quality_note: GitHub repository with specific training script path provided

semantic_analysis.format_details: GitHub URL well-formed; pipeline_url points to specific file path
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Both refs point to the same repo; consistent with training_configuration which references configs/resnet50_fpn.yaml in the same repo
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: Repo path layout (training/, configs/, inference/) is conventional

sub_elements.name: External Standards or References Cited
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.citations[0]: SC24 conference paper; quantitative_analysis.evaluation_procedure.baselines: references to 'Prabhat et al., 2021' and 'WeatherBench'
sub_elements.quality_note: Conference paper citation and references to comparison baselines provided

semantic_analysis.format_details: Narrative references to prior work but no DOI or arXiv ID attached
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: ClimateNet-v1 (Prabhat et al., 2021) is consistent with version.diff statement of upgrade from ResNet-34
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: Prabhat et al. ClimateNet and WeatherBench are real reference works in this field

elements.name: Performance Evaluation and Limitations Disclosure
elements.description: Does the metadata communicate performance, known risks, biases, and limitations?

sub_elements.name: Quantitative Performance Metrics Reported
sub_elements.evidence: quantitative_analysis.performance_metrics: 5 metrics (IoU for TC=0.847, AR=0.782, Fronts=0.691, Detection mAP@0.5=0.823, Inference throughput=125.3 patches/s) with slices
sub_elements.quality_note: Multiple quantitative metrics with numeric values and dataset slices

semantic_analysis.format_details: Each metric has type, value, and (where appropriate) slice + unit
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: All 4 quality metrics in [0,1] range; throughput uses sensible unit (patches/s on A100)
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: IoU values 0.69-0.85 are realistic for climate-event segmentation; mAP@0.5=0.82 plausible for detection of large meteorological structures

sub_elements.name: Performance Across Sub-populations / Slices
sub_elements.evidence: performance_metrics slices: 'Test set (E3SM v2)' for segmentation metrics, 'Test set (all events)' for detection, 'single A100 GPU' for throughput; limitations: 'degrades in polar regions (>70°)'
sub_elements.quality_note: Metrics reported with dataset slices and geographic performance variation documented

semantic_analysis.format_details: Slices populated on each performance_metric
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Slices vary by event-type but a per-region / per-hemisphere slice is missing despite documented polar-region performance degradation in limitations
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: Only one climate model (E3SM v2) appears in segmentation metric slices; cross-model variability across the 3 test models is not slice-reported

sub_elements.name: Confidence Intervals or Error Bars
sub_elements.evidence: performance_metrics[0-2].confidence_interval: TC IoU [0.841, 0.853], AR IoU [0.774, 0.790], Front IoU [0.681, 0.701]; evaluation_procedure.uncertainty_quantification: 'bootstrap resampling (10,000 iterations), ensemble σ=0.012'
sub_elements.quality_note: Confidence intervals provided for key metrics with bootstrap methodology

semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Detection mAP@0.5 and throughput lack CIs even though uncertainty methodology is documented
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: CI widths (±0.006-0.010) are plausible for n>10k bootstrap on segmentation IoU

sub_elements.name: Limitations Section Present
sub_elements.evidence: considerations.limitations: 3 concrete limitations (coarse resolution, atmospheric-only phenomena, polar region degradation)
sub_elements.quality_note: Multiple specific limitations with concrete failure modes

semantic_analysis.format_details: Each limitation has a substantive multi-line description
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Coarse-resolution limitation (≥15km) consistent with MPAS-A 15km training data; polar limitation consistent with out_of_scope_uses notes on observational adaptation
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: All 3 limitations correspond to known failure modes of climate-model-trained segmentation networks

sub_elements.name: Tradeoffs / Risks Acknowledged
sub_elements.evidence: considerations.tradeoffs: 2 quantified tradeoffs (accuracy vs speed: 3% IoU gain but 64% slower at higher resolution; model size vs accuracy: 40% faster but 5% IoU drop with smaller backbone)
sub_elements.quality_note: Quantified tradeoffs with specific performance numbers

semantic_analysis.format_details: Both tradeoffs include quantitative deltas (3% IoU, 45 vs 125 p/s, 40% speedup)
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Tradeoffs are purely engineering (accuracy/speed/size); a fairness-related tradeoff (accuracy vs polar-region coverage) is implied by limitations but not surfaced here
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: Higher-res throughput drop (125→45 p/s ≈ 64%) is consistent with O(N²) scaling for 1536² vs 768² patches

elements.name: Cross-Platform and Community Integration
elements.description: Does the model card connect to wider model ecosystems, benchmarks, and standards?

sub_elements.name: Published on a Recognized Platform
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.references: GitHub (https://github.com/lbnl-climate/climatenet-v2) and dedicated project site (https://climatenet.lbl.gov); library_name: 'climatenet'
sub_elements.quality_note: Published on GitHub with dedicated project landing page

semantic_analysis.format_details: Both URLs well-formed and use recognized platforms (GitHub, lbl.gov)
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: GitHub + institutional site present but no HuggingFace Hub, Zenodo, or Papers-with-Code presence — reduces ML-community discoverability
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io) also referenced in installation instructions, adding a distribution channel

sub_elements.name: Cross-referenced DOIs or Related Model Links
sub_elements.evidence: No DOI present in references or citations
sub_elements.quality_note: Conference paper cited but no DOI provided; no cross-references to parent/related models via DOI

semantic_analysis.format_details: No DOI string matching 10.XXXX/... pattern anywhere in the card
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: SC24 papers receive IEEE DOIs (prefix 10.1109/) — citation could and should include one; ClimateNet-v1 reference also has no DOI/arXiv ID
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: Lack of DOI/arXiv link materially reduces long-term citability and cross-platform indexing

sub_elements.name: Benchmark Results (Papers with Code style)
sub_elements.evidence: quantitative_analysis.evaluation_procedure: benchmarks with standard metrics (IoU, mAP), baselines (ClimateNet-v1, TECA, WeatherBench), sota_comparison with improvement percentages
sub_elements.quality_note: Benchmark-quality results with baselines and SOTA comparisons

semantic_analysis.format_details: Benchmark info present in evaluation_procedure but the structured model_index[] (Papers-with-Code style) is not used
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Baselines (ClimateNet-v1, TECA) and SOTA deltas align with version.diff claim of +15% TC accuracy
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: +8.6% IoU over ClimateNet-v1 and +15% over WeatherBench are plausible improvement magnitudes

sub_elements.name: Standards / Schema Conformance Stated
sub_elements.evidence: schema_version: '2.0.0-extended', follows LinkML model_card_schema structure
sub_elements.quality_note: Explicit schema version conformance stated

semantic_analysis.format_details: schema_version is a semver-like string with explicit '-extended' qualifier
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Card uses extended-template fields (mission_relevance, compute_infrastructure, reproducibility_info, usage_documentation) consistent with declared schema variant
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: Schema version matches this project's extended LinkML schema

sub_elements.name: Datasets Linked to Datasheets / D4D References
sub_elements.evidence: model_parameters.data: 3 datasets with names and links (https://www.cesm.ucar.edu/models/cam, https://e3sm.org, https://mpas-dev.github.io) but no datasheet references
sub_elements.quality_note: Dataset links provided but no datasheets (D4D) or formal dataset registry references

semantic_analysis.format_details: Links point to model-family homepages, not to dataset-specific records or datasheets
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Card is the base/extended variant (not d4dharmonized) so no Datasheet-for-Datasets reference structure is used; dataset provenance details (size, license, custodian) are not formally tracked
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: CESM/E3SM/MPAS-A homepages are real and authoritative for the model families but do not constitute datasheets for the specific simulation outputs used

format_failures.issue: Bias fields absent despite limitations documenting geographic bias in polar regions
Sensitive-data flag is omitted on all 3 dataset entries; should be explicitly set (likely false) rather than absent
No DOI provided for SC24 paper or ClimateNet-v1 reference; no arXiv ID either
Links point to climate-model homepages rather than to datasheets (D4D) or dataset-specific records
Citation provided in IEEE narrative style only; no BibTeX entry for machine ingestion
Base model (ResNet-50 ImageNet pretrained) described in prose but top-level base_model slot is not populated

consistency_failures.issue: Narrative description says 'standard SGD optimization' but hyperparameters specify 'AdamW' — internal contradiction
version.diff claims training on 12 climate models but only 3 are enumerated in model_parameters.data
input_format describes (768, 768, 16) channels-last layout while code example uses (16, 768, 768) channels-first PyTorch convention

consistency_failures.rule: limitations[polar bias] vs bias_model / quantitative_analysis slices
consistency_failures.issue: Geographic bias documented in limitations but not reflected in structured bias_* fields nor in per-region performance slices

consistency_failures.rule: training data vs evaluation benchmark models
consistency_failures.issue: E3SM v2 and MPAS-A appear in both training data and evaluation test set (split is by simulation year, which is legitimate but only GFDL CM4 represents a fully held-out model family)

plausibility_failures.issue: ORCIDs 0000-0002-1234-5678 and 0000-0003-9876-5432 look like sequential placeholder IDs rather than registered real-person ORCIDs (acceptable in an example card but flagged for production use)

strengths: Exceptional architecture documentation with layer-by-layer breakdown and 38.5M parameter count
Comprehensive compute infrastructure reporting: NERSC Perlmutter, 256 A100 GPUs, 32,768 GPU-hours with full hardware/software specs
Complete reproducibility artifacts: seed, hyperparameters, environment.yml reference, training script URL, SLURM configuration
Strong performance reporting with confidence intervals via bootstrap (10,000 iterations) across 5 metrics; all CI bounds correctly bracket the point estimates
Excellent provenance: 4 contributors with CRediT roles, ORCIDs, affiliations; version 2.0.0 with detailed diff
Clear DOE mission alignment with project name, facility, real funding source (BER grant DE-AC02-05CH11231 is LBNL's actual prime contract), and impact areas
Outstanding usage documentation: 3 complete code examples (basic inference, evaluation, batch processing), installation via conda/Docker/NERSC module, wget-able checkpoint
Explicit out-of-scope uses: real-time forecasting, satellite application, climate attribution
All quantitative metrics fall within plausible ranges (IoU in [0,1], CI bounds well-ordered)
weaknesses: Missing bias_model / bias_output formal disclosure despite limitations explicitly mentioning polar-region degradation
No sensitive_data_used field populated (likely N/A for climate model output but should be explicit)
No DOI provided for the SC24 paper or model itself (reduces cross-platform discoverability)
Dataset links point to model framework pages (CAM, E3SM, MPAS) but not to formal datasheets or registry entries
Narrative description of training contradicts structured hyperparameters (SGD vs AdamW)
version.diff says '12 climate models' but only 3 are enumerated in model_parameters.data
Input tensor layout in spec (HWC) does not match layout used in code example (CHW)
semantic_concerns: Train/test overlap at the model-family level: E3SM v2 and MPAS-A appear in both training and evaluation; only GFDL CM4 is a fully held-out model. Time-based split is legitimate but the card should be more explicit that within-model temporal holdout is being conflated with cross-model generalization
Geographic bias (polar regions >70°) documented in prose limitations but not surfaced in bias_model / bias_output structured fields nor as a performance slice — fairness assessment is incomplete
Optimizer inconsistency between training_procedure.description ('standard SGD') and reproducibility_info.hyperparameters.optimizer ('AdamW') would mislead a reproducer reading only the narrative
ORCID values appear to be sequential placeholders rather than real registered IDs (consistent with this being an example template card)
recommendations: Add bias_model disclosure: 'Geographic bias: Performance degrades in polar regions (>70° latitude) due to limited training data at high latitudes. IoU drops by ~X% for events above 70°N/S.'
Populate sensitive.sensitive_data_used=false for each dataset in model_parameters.data to explicitly confirm no sensitive data
Register model on Zenodo or similar to obtain a DOI; add to model_details.references
Add DOI (10.1109/...) to SC24 paper citation once published
Create or link to datasheets for the 3 climate model datasets (CAM5.1, E3SM v2, MPAS-A) following D4D template; consider migrating to the d4dharmonized schema variant
Reconcile training_procedure.description (currently says 'SGD optimization') with hyperparameters.optimizer='AdamW' — replace 'SGD' with 'AdamW-based gradient descent' in the prose
Resolve discrepancy between version.diff (12 climate models) and enumerated model_parameters.data (3 entries) — either expand data[] or correct the diff text
Align input_format declared layout (HWC) with the layout actually used in code_examples (CHW)
Add a per-region (e.g. polar / mid-latitude / tropical) performance slice to quantitative_analysis.performance_metrics to make the documented geographic bias quantitatively visible
Add a fairness-related tradeoff entry (accuracy vs geographic coverage) to considerations.tradeoffs
Replace placeholder ORCIDs with real registered IDs before non-example deployment
Add a BibTeX-formatted entry alongside the IEEE-style citation to support automated reference managers
Populate the top-level base_model field with the ResNet-50 ImageNet checkpoint reference for explicit lineage tracking
